title: pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. glauca
intro: Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. glauca, also known as Rocky Mountain Douglas Fir, is a subspecies of coniferous tree native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its blue-green needles and slender branches. The bark and needles of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. glauca are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to create topical creams and salves for skin conditions and injuries.
